title: Nat Ford aerial processing contract (natford-aerial-processing.v1)
description: Service-level contract between a planning application (e.g. OpenPlan) and the Aerial Intel Platform acting as its ODM processing backend. The consumer POSTs a ProcessingRequest to the platform's external processing endpoint; the platform processes the imagery through its NodeODM pipeline and POSTs ProcessingCallback payloads to the consumer's callbackUrl as the job advances. Both directions authenticate with a pre-shared bearer token. On a 'succeeded' callback the consumer writes its evidence record (OpenPlan: an aerial_evidence_packages row) from the artifacts list. This file is committed identically to both repositories; treat it as the single source of truth and bump schemaVersion for any breaking change.

requestId.description: Caller-generated idempotency key. Retried submissions with the same requestId must not create a second job.

callbackUrl.description: HTTPS endpoint on the consumer that receives ProcessingCallback payloads. Bearer-authenticated with the shared callback token.

url.description: Signed, time-limited URL to a ZIP of source images. Must remain valid long enough for the platform's ingest cron to pull it.

presetId.description: Processing preset on the platform's NodeODM adapter.

callbackId.description: Unique per delivery; consumers must dedupe on it (redeliveries are allowed).

status.description: Job lifecycle status, aligned with the platform's drone_processing_jobs.status vocabulary.

artifacts.description: Present when status is 'succeeded'. Each entry is a processed output the consumer can pull to build its evidence record.

kind.description: Canonical output kinds from the platform's real-output adapter.

benchmarkSummary.description: The platform's benchmark_summary.json for the run (schema owned by the platform; pass through opaque).
